name: Mirage JS
description: Client-side mock API library for JavaScript apps that lets frontend teams build, test and share complete applications without any backend.

seo.title: Mirage JS — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Mirage JS: Client-side mock API library for JavaScript apps that lets frontend teams build, test and share complete applications without any backend.

when_to_use: Frontend development with mocked backend API responses
Test React/Vue apps without any real backend running
Share complete app prototypes without backend dependency
Reproducible API states for deterministic UI tests

pros: Ship frontend without waiting on backend APIs
Models, factories and serializers feel like Rails
Runs entirely in the browser for fast iteration
Great for demos and offline development
cons: Bundled into app builds, easy to ship by mistake
MSW covers similar ground more flexibly
Extra abstraction some teams never use
review.verdict: Still a nice DX for frontend-only flows, but MSW has largely eaten its lunch for new projects.
review.best_for: Frontend teams prototyping UIs before backend endpoints exist
review.not_for: New projects that should adopt MSW for broader mocking coverage
